data.title: Hing Yip unit extends and adjusts finance leases for Henan sewage assets
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

China Investments Holdings Limited ( (HK:0132) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Hing Yip Holdings’ subsidiary Greengold Leasing has signed adjustment agreements with a lessee to modify existing finance leases over sewage treatment and conveyance equipment in Pingdingshan, Henan, extending the lease terms by two years to 2030. The arrangements keep the lessee responsible for maintenance, taxes and related costs on the assets.

Under the revised terms, the total outstanding lease principal on the existing finance leases stands at about RMB16.9 million, with Finance Lease 1 to have outstanding payments of roughly RMB11.6 million, including principal and interest, to be repaid quarterly over the extended period. As one of the applicable percentage ratios for these transactions exceeds 5% but remains below 25%, the move is classified as a discloseable transaction under Hong Kong listing rules, signalling a material but mid-sized adjustment to the group’s leasing portfolio and cash-flow profile.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0132) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.21 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Investments Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:0132 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Investments Holdings Limited

Hing Yip Holdings Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates through subsidiaries including Greengold Leasing to provide finance leasing services. Its activities include leasing and leaseback arrangements for infrastructure-related assets, such as sewage treatment and conveyance equipment in mainland China, serving corporate clients that require long-term asset financing solutions.

China Dongxiang (Group) Co ( (HK:3818) ) just unveiled an announcement.

China Dongxiang has announced the current composition of its board of directors, naming Chen Yihong as chairman, Chen Chen as chief executive officer and co-chairman, and Lyu Guanghong as chief financial officer, alongside three independent non-executive directors. The company also detailed the membership and chairmanship of its five key board committees — Executive, Audit, Remuneration, Nomination, and Environmental, Social and Governance — underscoring its formalised governance framework and distribution of oversight responsibilities among executive and independent directors.

These committee assignments highlight an emphasis on structured oversight in areas such as financial reporting, remuneration policy, board appointments, and ESG matters, which are crucial for compliance and investor confidence in a Hong Kong-listed issuer. By clarifying roles and functions across the board and its committees, China Dongxiang provides stakeholders with greater transparency into its decision-making processes and the checks and balances supporting management accountability.

Tian Ge Interactive Holdings has announced that its Independent Financial Adviser maintains its previous view that the conditional voluntary cash partial offer by Sina Hong Kong Limited to acquire 32,500,000 of the company’s shares is fair and reasonable for independent shareholders. data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Add New Energy Investment Holdings Group Ltd. ( (HK:2623) ).

Add New Energy Investment Holdings Group Ltd. has agreed to acquire 37.7 million shares in Australian-listed Horizon (HRZ), equal to about 14.46% of its issued share capital, for A$40.7 million, to be paid by issuing 46.78 million new Add New Energy shares under its general mandate at HK$4.70 each. Together with a prior subscription for up to 36.6 million HRZ shares to support HRZ’s gold processing hub near Kalgoorlie, the aggregated transactions constitute a discloseable transaction under Hong Kong listing rules, and the company has applied for resumption of trading in its shares following a suspension pending this announcement.

The acquisition further deepens Add New Energy’s exposure to HRZ’s gold development project in Western Australia and signals a strategic bet on gold processing infrastructure. By settling the consideration entirely in shares, the company preserves cash while diluting existing shareholders, and the classification as a discloseable transaction underscores the growing financial significance of its stake in HRZ for investors monitoring its capital allocation and resource-sector strategy.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2623) stock is a Hold with a HK$4.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Add New Energy Investment Holdings Group Ltd. stock, see the HK:2623 Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Tian Ge Interactive Swings to Loss Despite Surging Revenue in 2025
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Tian Ge Interactive Holdings ( (HK:1980) ) has shared an announcement.

Tian Ge Interactive reported a sharp rise in revenue to RMB38.6 million in 2025 from RMB10.2 million a year earlier, driven mainly by growth in its online interactive entertainment services. However, gross margin fell from 79.8% to 55.2%, and the company swung from a net profit of RMB19.9 million in 2024 to a net loss of RMB13.3 million in 2025, with adjusted metrics also turning negative.

The deterioration in profitability, including a move to negative adjusted EBITDA despite stable total assets and modestly lower liabilities, suggests rising costs or changing revenue mix are pressuring margins as the business scales. This shift may raise concerns for investors about the sustainability of Tian Ge’s growth strategy and its ability to convert expanding top-line performance in online entertainment into consistent earnings and cash generation.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1980) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.77 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Tian Ge Interactive Holdings stock, see the HK:1980 Stock Forecast page.

More about Tian Ge Interactive Holdings

Tian Ge Interactive Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the online interactive entertainment industry. The Group generates most of its revenue from online interactive entertainment services, supplemented by other related activities, and focuses on digital platforms that monetize user engagement in mainland China and broader online markets.

Shanghai Pioneer Holding’s subsidiary has signed an exclusive long-term distribution agreement with Italian-based LABO INTERNATIONAL, a sister company of Switzerland’s Labo Cosprophar AG, to sell and promote the “LABO HAIR LOSS” anti-hair loss vials in mainland China. The topical cosmetic product, developed by the Swiss hair and skincare brand Labo, is designed to reduce physiological and non-pathological hair loss and has secured Chinese regulatory approval as a special cosmetic, supported by clinical trial data on safety and efficacy.

The company positions this exclusive right as strategically important amid rising hair loss concerns and surging demand for effective anti-hair loss and hair-strengthening solutions in China, and plans to deploy its Beauty Division to drive marketing and channel expansion. Management expects that building out distribution and consumer awareness for the LABO HAIR LOSS line will enhance the group’s presence in the premium hair care segment while aiming to deliver improved customer experience and long-term value for shareholders.

Shanghai Pioneer Holding Ltd, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates a Beauty Division focused on skincare and personal care products for the Chinese market. The group targets fast-growing consumer health and beauty segments in the People’s Republic of China, leveraging partnerships with international brands to expand its product portfolio and market reach.

Dah Sing Banking Group ( (HK:2356) ) has issued an update.

Dah Sing Banking Group reported a strong set of results for the year ended 31 December 2025, with net interest income rising 10.2% to HK$5.83 billion and net fee and commission income jumping 28.1%, driving total operating income up 14.1%. Operating profit before impairment losses increased 24.3%, while credit impairment losses were broadly stable, leading to a 20.2% rise in profit attributable to shareholders to HK$2.48 billion and higher basic earnings per share.

Despite one-off headwinds including a HK$493 million impairment loss on goodwill and a net loss from investment property revaluations, the group’s overall profitability improved, supported by contributions from associates and jointly controlled entities. The board also lifted total dividends for the year, signalling confidence in the bank’s capital position and earnings resilience, which may be viewed positively by investors and other stakeholders.

data.title: Shenzhou International Grows 2025 Revenue but Profit Hit by FX Losses
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shenzhou International Group Holdings ( (HK:2313) ) has shared an update.

Shenzhou International Group Holdings reported 2025 sales of about RMB30.99 billion, up 8.1% year on year, driven mainly by robust demand for sportswear in the United States and Europe and strong growth in casual wear sales in Japan and other markets. Sportswear accounted for 67.7% of revenue, casual wear 27.1% and lingerie 4.5%, with lingerie sales slipping on weaker Japanese demand.

Despite higher revenue and a 1.4% rise in gross profit to roughly RMB8.17 billion, the gross margin narrowed by 1.8 percentage points to 26.3%, while net profit after tax fell 6.7% to about RMB5.83 billion, weighed by the absence of a prior-year disposal gain and foreign-exchange losses from renminbi appreciation. The board proposed a total 2025 dividend of HKD2.58 per share, up around 2.0% and implying a payout ratio of about 60.9%, signalling continued commitment to shareholder returns despite profit pressure.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2313) stock is a Buy with a HK$70.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Shenzhou International Group Holdings stock, see the HK:2313 Stock Forecast page.

More about Shenzhou International Group Holdings

Shenzhou International Group Holdings is a leading textile and garment manufacturer based in Greater China, specialising in sportswear, casual wear and lingerie for global brand customers. The group is heavily exposed to demand trends in key export markets including the United States, Europe and Japan, where shifts in consumer appetite directly influence its product mix and revenue growth.
